steps: Go to https://supabase.com/dashboard and log in or create a free account
Create a new project by clicking 'New project', entering a name, database password, and region
Wait for the project to finish provisioning (about 1-2 minutes)
Go to your project's Settings > API (or visit https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/YOUR_PROJECT_REF/settings/api)
Under 'Project API keys', copy the 'anon public' key for client-side access or the 'service_role secret' key for server-side admin access
Also copy your Project URL (e.g., https://YOUR_PROJECT_REF.supabase.co) — this is your actual API base URL

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key (anon or service_role)
credential_fields.description: Use the 'anon' key for public client-side access (respects Row Level Security) or the 'service_role' key for admin/server-side access (bypasses RLS).

notes: Free tier available (2 active projects, 500 MB database, 1 GB file storage)
The actual API base URL is https://YOUR_PROJECT_REF.supabase.co — not api.supabase.io
The 'anon' key is safe for client-side use when Row Level Security (RLS) is enabled
The 'service_role' key bypasses all security policies — never expose it in client-side code
API keys are JWTs and can be viewed at any time in the project dashboard

test_endpoint.description: Fetch the PostgREST OpenAPI spec to verify the API key and project URL
